2604.19730.title: FASTER: Value-Guided Sampling for Fast RL
2604.19730.paper_summary: Some of the most performant reinforcement learning algorithms today can be prohibitively expensive as they use test-time scaling methods such as sampling multiple action candidates and selecting the best one. In this work, we propose FASTER, a method for getting the benefits of sampling-based test-time scaling of diffusion-based policies without the computational cost by tracing the performance gain of action samples back to earlier in the denoising process. Our key insight is that we can model the denoising of multiple action candidates and selecting the best one as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) where the goal is to progressively filter action candidates before denoising is complete. With this MDP, we can learn a policy and value function in the denoising space that predicts the downstream value of action candidates in the denoising process and filters them while maximizing returns. The result is a method that is lightweight and can be plugged into existing generative RL algorithms. Across challenging long-horizon manipulation tasks in online and batch-online RL, FASTER consistently improves the underlying policies and achieves the best overall performance among the compared methods. Applied to a pretrained VLA, FASTER achieves the same performance while substantially reducing training and inference compute requirements. Code is available at https://github.com/alexanderswerdlow/faster .
2604.19730.paper_summary_zh: 一些當今最具效能的強化學習算法可能會非常昂貴，因為它們使用測試時縮放方法，例如抽樣多個行動候選並選擇最佳者。在這項工作中，我們提出了FASTER，一種在不增加計算成本的情況下，獲得基於擴散政策的抽樣時縮放優勢的方法，通過追蹤行動樣本的性能增益回溯到去噪過程的早期。我們的關鍵見解是，我們可以將多個行動候選的去噪和選擇最佳者建模為一個馬爾可夫決策過程（MDP），其目標是在去噪完成之前逐步篩選行動候選。通過這個MDP，我們可以在去噪空間中學習一個策略和價值函數，預測行動候選在去噪過程中的下游價值，並在最大化回報的同時篩選它們。結果是一種輕量級的方法，可以插入現有的生成式強化學習算法。在在線和批量在線強化學習中的挑戰性長期操作任務中，FASTER始終改善基礎策略，並在比較方法中實現最佳的整體性能。應用於預訓練的VLA，FASTER在顯著降低訓練和推理計算需求的同時達到相同的性能。代碼可在 https://github.com/alexanderswerdlow/faster 獲得。
2604.19730.author: Perry Dong et.al.
2604.19730.authors: Perry Dong, Alexander Swerdlow, Dorsa Sadigh, Chelsea Finn
